notes: words(list[,sep])/first(list[,sep])/rest(list[,sep]) -- sep defaults to space, validated the same as trim/ljust/rjust/iter's fill-char arg. Zero args (true empty parens, not an empty-string arg) special-cases before the normal arg-count check: words() -> 0, first()/rest() -> empty string. words()/first() are full-list-split element count/first-element, same collapse-vs-non-collapse split rule as iter() (default space collapses runs and trims; custom single-char sep does a plain non-collapsing split). rest() is the subtle one: NOT the same as re-joining splitCompressed(list)[1..] -- functions.c's fun_rest does a single trim_space_sep + split_token call, which trims the leading/trailing run once and collapses only the separator run immediately following the first token, leaving every other interior run in the remainder raw/uncollapsed. That subtlety is invisible for plain typed-in multiple spaces though -- the general argument evaluator's own space compression (mudconf.space_compress, unrelated to fun_rest's own logic) already collapses them before rest() ever sees the string, which is why rest(a  b   c) oracle-verifies to "b c" same as the fully-collapsed case. Only backslash-escaped spaces (bypassing that pre-compression) reveal the real per-call behavior: rest(a\ \ b\ \ \ c) oracle-verifies to "b   c" (the later three-space run survives), not "b c".
